family watching crab demonstration on boat overlooking water and forest.

A black and white tour boat named “Aleutian Ballad” sails through calm waters with forested mountains in the background. The vessel is labeled “Bering Sea Crab Fishermen’s Tour” and is filled with bundled-up passengers on the lower deck, likely enjoying a narrated wildlife or fishing experience near the Alaskan coast. Small houses line the forested shoreline behind the boat.

restrictions.restrictionDisplayText: Are you greater than or equal to 5.0 years ?

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Bering Sea Crab Fishermen's Tour

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 60-79% booked

commerceProducts.description: Set out to sea aboard the Aleutian Ballad, a Bering Sea crab boat, for an adventure that’s so carefree even landlubbers can do it. This actual boat was featured on season two of the Discovery Channel series, Deadliest Catch, and it’s seen some serious rolling seas and massive rogue waves. Fortunately, that won’t happen today. Instead, you’ll cruise calm waters as the fishing crew demonstrates the art of crabbing and long line fishing. Watch as massive crab pots are hauled on deck. Plus, check out the unique octopus pots, and keep your eyes peeled as long lines are pulled from the deep to see what might be on the other end. During the voyage, you’re also likely to spot wildlife, including majestic Bald Eagles, so keep those cameras ready. This award-winning excursion gives you a fun, safe and easy taste of the hard-earned life on the Bering Sea.
highlights: Enjoy stadium-style seating on your quest across the Inside Passage.
Discover amazing wildlife, including eagles, crabs, and octopus.
Learn about commercial fishing and the fishermen's unique adventures.

source.altText: Sled dogs taking off pulling man.
group of people with sled dogs.
view from the train on the White Pass Rail.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Goldrush Sled Dogs and White Pass Train Adventure

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 80-100% booked

commerceProducts.description: On this tour, you’ll take Skagway’s famous White Pass and Yukon Route Railroad along the eastern side of the White Pass to the 2,865-foot summit. Journey down the west side of White Pass canyon in the comfort of a motorcoach passing several landmarks including Dead Horse Gulch, Pitchfork Falls, and the beautiful Tormented Valley. Along the way, stop at Alaska 360 Dredge Town to learn more about modern dog sled racing before meeting a team of sled dogs.
highlights: Experience an unforgettable journey to the White Pass Summit aboard the historic White Pass and Yukon Route Railroad.
Visit Alaska 360 Dredge Town to learn about Alaska's state sport and watch a team of Iditarod sled dogs race.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require extended periods of walking over even and uneven surfaces, steep terrain and/or water activity in a slight current. source.altText: trees in a forest.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Rainforest Wildlife Sanctuary, Eagles and Totems
commerceProducts.description: Delve into the heart of Ketchikan's rainforest on a scenic eco-tour at an exclusive reserve, led by a naturalist. Begin your day at Herring Cover, following your naturalist along a nature trail through dense forest and across tall stands of hemlock, spruce, and Alaska cedar. Enjoy stops at picture-perfect viewing areas along the way. At Eagle Creek, a protected estuary, keep an eye out for local wildlife, including bears and seals during salmon spawning season. Also watch for mink, Marten, and wolves.
highlights: Embark on a scenic eco-tour, complete with a nature hike a native totem carver, totem park and historic sawmill.
Follow Eagle Creek at an open estuary along an elevated wooden boardwalk overlooking grassy wetlands.
Visit Herring Cove, home to eagles, Black Bears, seals, and a variety of other species.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.
activityLevel.name: Mild

source.altText: eagle on a tree from distance.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Lighthouse, Totems and Eagles
commerceProducts.description: Your Alaskan captain and their crew will share Southeast Alaska's coastal history, Native culture, and natural beauty as you journey into Ketchikan's wild seascape on a wildlife-viewing cruise, with guaranteed sightings of American bald eagles. Discover the beauty of the Tongass National Forest, the world's largest temperate rainforest, and learn about Alaska's interconnected ecosystems and the animals that call this region home. Enjoy panoramic views of Totem Bight State Park from the water, including an Alaska Native clan house and a collection of 14 towering Tlingit and Haida hand-carved cedar totem poles. Breathe in the fresh sea air as you cruise to Guard Island, where you'll have the opportunity to photograph the first lighthouse to be built in Alaska, completed in 1904 and still in operation today. Sip a hot chocolate and keep on the lookout for harbor seals, moon jellyfish, pigeon guillemots, deer, blue heron, whales, and other wildlife.
highlights: Embark on an award-winning wildlife-viewing cruise where you'll enjoy guaranteed sightings of American bald eagles.
Take a scenic drive through the heart of Ketchikan.
Sip a warm hot chocolate supreme and savor a complimentary tasting of Alaskan hors d’oeuvres, including a traditional, alder-smoked salmon.
Marvel at the beauty of the Alaskan Native clan house and hand-carved cedar totem poles at Totem Bight State Historic Park
Enjoy panoramic views of the Tongass National Forest, Vallenar Rocks and its resident wildlife, and the historic Guard Island Lighthouse

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Taku Glacier Helicopter and Airboat Adventure
commerceProducts.description: Venture into the heart of Alaska’s wilderness by air, water, and ice! This is a rare opportunity to experience the Taku Glacier from all possible perspectives. Begin your adventure with an aerial exploration of the breathtaking Juneau Icefield and surrounding Tongass National Forest before touching down at the isolated Taku Point. Continue with an hour-long airboat expedition of the area and experience close-up views of the Taku Glacier as you cruise its 5-mile terminus. Next, fly up to the surface of the icefield for your brief glacier landing With viewing opportunities of the glacier’s dramatic face and deep blue crevasses, this is sure to be a truly unforgettable Alaskan experience.
highlights: Learn about the Alaska wilderness from a knowledgeable guide as you fly above the Tongass National Forest.
Land on the Taku Glacier, the thickest and deepest of the word's glaciers, accessible only by air and water.
Face the glacier head on and close up from an airboat in the Taku River.

source.altText: A glistening sunset with the double decker cruise sailing through the Juneau, Alaska waters.
pod of humpback whales off the coast of Juneau.
Whale breaching with a stunning sunset over the mountains in Juneau, Alaska.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Exclusive Alaskan Whale Discovery
commerceProducts.description: It's a seafaring safari! Watch humpback whales, Steller sea lions, Dall's porpoise, orcas and harbor seals up close on this small group experience. A safari vessel, low to the water for a favorable vantage point, hosts fully guided, small-group whale-watching adventures on a voyage through Stephens Passage, a waterway known for plentiful whale activity. A professional naturalist guide leads the group through hands-on activities that support research into the entire ecosystem, such as monitoring for invasive-species, humpback-whale identification and marine mammal observations. Pull a crab pot with your guide to explore and learn more about the critters roaming around Juneau’s Ocean floor as this data is used to give back to local research and conservation efforts. The time in Stephens Passage provides a transformative opportunity not only to see whales and enjoy their antics, but to deepen one's understanding of delicate marine environments and the creatures that exist in them.
highlights: Become a Citizen Scientist by assisting in important data collecting tasks such as a crab pot pull to monitor Juneau's ocean floor for invasive species
An exclusive experience on a smaller-sized whale-watching vessel allows you to get up close with Alaska's wild beauty.
See all manner of marine and bird life, including sea lions, porpoise, gulls, guillemots, as you cruise through beautiful Stephens Passage.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require extended periods of walking over even and uneven surfaces, steep terrain and/or water activity in a slight current. source.altText: a misty lake surrounded by a forest.

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Mendenhall Lake Canoe
commerceProducts.description: Paddle across Mendenhall Lake, navigating past icebergs, surrounded by Alaskan wildlife on an exceptionally stable, 28-foot, traditional Tlingit-style canoe, with all modern safety gear supplied. Venture within a safe distance of the awe-inspiring face of the Mendenhall Glacier and the beautiful Nugget Falls. Then end your day with an Alaskan-style snack. Due to the close approach to the glacier face, the Forest Service tightly controls the number of participants. Be one of the lucky few to experience this one-of-a-kind adventure.
highlights: Venture within a safe distance of the massive, awe-inspiring face of the living Mendenhall Glacier.
Paddle across Mendenhall Lake, navigating past icebergs, surrounded by one of the richest samplings of Alaskan wildlife.
Grab a thrill ride by skirting the base of the beautiful and powerful Nugget Falls and enjoy an Alaskan-style snack.

activityLevel.description: Full participation may require leisurely walking over primarily even surfaces. There may be a limited number of steps, a minimal amount of cobblestones or uneven surfaces, and some standing for extended periods of time.
activityLevel.name: Mild

a river flowing through the snow.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Mendenhall Glacier, Salmon Hatchery and Tram

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 60-79% booked

commerceProducts.description: Experience the big chill of the splendid Mendenhall Glacier, discover the bustling activity of a salmon hatchery, and get to know the colorful cultural heritage of Juneau, where the Goldbelt Tram takes you to the top of Mount Roberts to see it all from above.
highlights: Make your way to Mendenhall Glacier, the region's largest most accessible glacier, for free time taking in the splendor of the mighty glacier, which features a 13-mile-long river of ice and is one of several that connects to the awesome Juneau Ice Field.
Visit a salmon hatchery that harvests millions of salmon each year for a narrated tour, touch tanks, and detailed marine exhibits that explain the salmon lifecycle.

Return to downtown Juneau and the Goldbelt Tram to ascend to the top of Mount Roberts and experience 360-degree views of the Gastineau Channel and Juneau, once home to Native American tribes and thousands of prospectors during the Gold Rush.

restrictions.restrictionDisplayText: Are you greater than or equal to 12.0 years ?

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Last Frontier Food Tour
commerceProducts.description: Eat your way through Juneau and you'll come to know Alaska's capital city in a new and meaningful way. Your culinary host, who is a local resident, will share the best of Alaskan food, as well as local legend and lore, on this leisurely walking tour through the culinary heart of the city. Stops include award-winning restaurants and you will often meet the chefs and restaurant owners who are making Juneau a sought-after dining destination. Your menu includes king crab bisque, fresh salmon, Alaskan game, the famous hog wing, and tea from indigenous plants, earning the "Made in Alaska" seal of approval. Your experience wraps up at an historic bar, where you will sample three beers from a local brewery. This experience focuses on connecting you with the city and with Alaska, and you're sure to come away with a deeper understanding and tasty memories of your time in Juneau.

activityLevel.description: This tour is not recommended for guests with pre-existing medical conditions such as cardiac, respiratory, or orthopedic conditions, including joint injuries or disease, or any other serious medical condition. Guests must consider their physical fitness level and medical history when determining whether this tour is appropriate. Guests who have concerns must consult with their personal physician before engaging in any activity that may be strenuous for them, particularly those involving water-based activities. Guests should consider their swimming ability when selecting tours involving water-based activities.
activityLevel.name: Strenuous

source.altText: a person going down the zipline.

restrictions.restrictionDisplayText: Are you greater than or equal to 6.0 years ?

commerceProducts.title: Small Group: Last Frontier Zip Lines and Adventure Park
commerceProducts.description: Fly high over the Alaskan rainforest canopy on this adrenaline-packed zip line adventure. Your journey begins with a scenic drive through Skagway. Travel up into the mountains on the historic Klondike Highway. Then, at the nature park, enjoy a short rainforest hike, arriving at the thrilling attractions. Meet your expert guides, gear up, and get ready to fly. Soar high over the rainforest canopy on six exhilarating zip lines. Scramble over a series of Burma loops, cross harrowing suspension bridges, and challenge yourself to walk over the raised balance beams. Test yourself with the ultimate challenge at the park and “walk the plank.” Fly over a zip line that soars where only the eagles dare. After your zip line adventure, warm yourself with a cup of hot cocoa, tea, coffee, and dispensable water will be available as well.
highlights: o Experience walking on raised balance beams, crossing suspension bridges, and soaring through the air on multiple zip lines.
o Drive through downtown Skagway and along the Klondike Highway to the starting point of your nature hike.
o Zip on the "Walk the Plank" line that soars high above the ground through the Alaskan treetops!

commerceProducts.title: Station One Distillery and Bottle Infusion Experience

promotion.title: CEL Shorex Evergreen Destination Highlights 0-59% booked

commerceProducts.description: Dive into the fascinating world of spirits with this hands-on distilling shore excursion, where you experience the art and science of crafting your own signature bottle of gin, vodka, whiskey, or agave spirits.
highlights: After a short walk from port, begin your journey with a warm welcome at Station One Distillery, where you meet your expert guide and fellow spirit enthusiasts.
The tour kicks off with a sneak peek behind the scenes of the distilling process, showcasing the state-of-the-art equipment and techniques that transform raw ingredients into premium spirits.
With your newfound knowledge, time to head upstairs to the distillery lab for the hands-on portion of your experience.
Choose from a curated selection of botanicals, herbs, spices, flowers, and fruits to design a flavor profile that suits your palate.

Once your blend is complete, bottle your private reserve. Watch as your creation is infused, sealed, and ready for its finishing touch—a custom label. Personalize it with your name, a clever tagline, or a meaningful message that captures the essence of your spirit.
Before concluding your Station One Distillery and Bottle Infusion excursion, your recipe is saved on file, ensuring you can reorder your signature creation for years to come.
Take a moment to celebrate your accomplishment with fellow participants and raise a glass to the unforgettable journey into distilling.

source.altText: a cable car descending in Jeneau, Alaska.

commerceProducts.title: Destination Highlight: Goldbelt Tram
commerceProducts.description: Enjoy a tram ride 1,800 feet into the Alaskan rainforest with stunning views of surrounding mountains and Silver Bow Basin.
highlights: Ascend 1,800 feet by tramcar through the Alaskan rainforest, taking in sweeping views of the Chilkat Mountains, Stephens Passage, Douglas Island, and historic Silver Bow Basin.
Explore the Mountain House, including the T’rale House for information on Mount Roberts trails and local wildlife.
Browse the Goldbelt Tram Gifts & Gallery, offering a wide selection of Alaskan gifts and souvenirs.
Watch the complimentary film “Seeing Daylight” at the Chilkat Theater, showcasing the culture of the Tlingit people; plays every 30 minutes.
Visit Timberline Bar & Grill, a full‑service restaurant located across from the theater; food and beverages are at guests' own expense.
